archery.description: Covers the use of bows, crossbows, and similar muscle-powered projectile weapons.
archery.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Archery [Accuracy] against the target's ranged defence. Add net hits to the weapon's Damage Value, then the target resists damage normally.

Use For
  • Bows and crossbows.
  • Called shots and other ranged-combat options when allowed.

automatics.description: Covers automatic firearms such as machine pistols, submachine guns, and assault rifles.
automatics.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Automatics [Accuracy] against the target's ranged defence.

Use For
  • Machine pistols, submachine guns, and assault rifles.
  • Single Shot, Semi-Automatic, Burst Fire, and Full-Auto modes when supported by the weapon.

blades.description: Covers edged melee weapons such as knives, swords, and axes.
blades.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Blades [Accuracy] against Reaction + Intuition. Add net hits to the weapon's Damage Value before the target resists damage.

Use For
Knives, swords, axes, and other edged melee weapons.

clubs.description: Covers blunt melee weapons such as batons, hammers, saps, and staves.
clubs.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Clubs [Accuracy] against Reaction + Intuition. Add net hits to the weapon's Damage Value before resistance.

Use For
Batons, hammers, saps, staves, and other blunt melee weapons.

exotic melee weapon.description: Covers one specific unusual melee weapon that is not governed by another standard combat skill.
exotic melee weapon.use:
Specialisation Required
This skill applies to one specific exotic melee weapon. Each different weapon requires its own Exotic Melee Weapon skill.

Typical Test
Roll Agility + Exotic Melee Weapon [Accuracy] against Reaction + Intuition.

Defaulting
This skill cannot be defaulted.

exotic ranged weapon.description: Covers one specific unusual ranged weapon that is not governed by another standard ranged-combat skill.
exotic ranged weapon.use:
Specialisation Required
This skill applies to one specific exotic ranged weapon. Each different weapon requires its own Exotic Ranged Weapon skill.

Typical Test
Roll Agility + Exotic Ranged Weapon [Accuracy] against the target's ranged defence.

Defaulting
This skill cannot be defaulted.

heavy weapons.description: Covers large military weapons such as assault cannons, grenade launchers, machine guns, rockets, and missiles.
heavy weapons.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Heavy Weapons [Accuracy] against the target's ranged defence.

Use For
  • Assault cannons and machine guns.
  • Grenade, rocket, and missile launchers.
Note
Explosive weapons may use scatter and blast rules instead of a direct damage resolution.

longarms.description: Covers shotguns, sporting rifles, and sniper rifles.
longarms.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Longarms [Accuracy] against the target's ranged defence. Add net hits to Damage Value before resistance.

Use For
Shotguns, sporting rifles, and sniper rifles.

pistols.description: Covers hold-out pistols, revolvers, semi-automatic pistols, and tasers.
pistols.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Pistols [Accuracy] against the target's ranged defence.

Use For
Hold-outs, light pistols, heavy pistols, and tasers.

throwing weapons.description: Covers accurately throwing grenades, knives, shuriken, and other hand-thrown weapons.
throwing weapons.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Throwing Weapons [Physical] to attack with thrown weapons.

Use For
  • Knives, shuriken, and other thrown weapons.
  • Grenades and similar explosives, which also use scatter and blast rules.

unarmed combat.description: Covers fighting without a conventional weapon, including strikes, blocks, grappling, and subduing attacks.
unarmed combat.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Unarmed Combat [Physical] against Reaction + Intuition. Add net hits to the attack's Damage Value before resistance.

Use For
Punches, kicks, natural weapons, and other unarmed attacks.

disguise.description: Covers altering a person’s visible appearance through clothing, cosmetics, prosthetics, camouflage, and similar techniques.
disguise.use:
Typical Test
Roll Intuition + Disguise [Mental] to create or maintain a disguise. Observers may oppose it with a suitable Perception test.

Use For
  • Changing visible appearance.
  • Concealing identifying features.
  • Using clothing, cosmetics, prosthetics, and disguise kits.

diving.description: Covers underwater movement and operation using diving equipment in varied aquatic conditions.
diving.use:
Typical Test
Roll Body + Diving [Physical] when difficult underwater conditions require a test.

Use For
  • Operating underwater safely.
  • Managing depth, currents, equipment, and extended dives.

escape artist.description: Covers escaping from restraints and bindings through flexibility, technique, and physical control.
escape artist.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Escape Artist [Physical] to slip restraints or escape confinement. The test may be opposed by the person who applied the restraint or made against a threshold set by its quality.

Use For
Ropes, cuffs, zip ties, contortion, and similar restraints.

free-fall.description: Covers controlling falls and safely using parachutes, rappelling equipment, ziplines, and similar descent methods.
free-fall.use:
Typical Test
Roll Body + Free-Fall [Physical] when controlling a dangerous descent or using equipment such as parachutes.

Use For
  • Parachuting and controlled falls.
  • Rappelling and similar rapid descents.
  • Reducing complications from difficult landings when the situation allows.

gymnastics.description: Covers balance, climbing, jumping, tumbling, parkour, and other acrobatic movement.
gymnastics.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Gymnastics [Physical] for acrobatics, balance, climbing, jumping, and similar movement challenges.

Combat Use
Gymnastics may also be used by certain defensive actions or movement tests when the rules allow it.

palming.description: Covers sleight of hand, concealing small objects, pickpocketing, and other subtle manual tricks.
palming.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Palming [Physical] to conceal, plant, or secretly manipulate small objects. Observers normally oppose this with Intuition + Perception [Mental].

Use For
Pickpocketing, sleight of hand, hiding objects on your person, and planting items unnoticed.

perception.description: Covers noticing and interpreting details through sight, hearing, smell, touch, taste, and other available senses.
perception.use:
Typical Test
Roll Intuition + Perception [Mental]. Use a threshold for hidden details or an Opposed Test against skills such as Sneaking or Palming.

Modifiers
Visibility, sound, distance, distraction, sensors, and other environmental conditions may change the dice pool or threshold.

running.description: Covers sprinting, distance running, and efficient movement on foot.
running.use:
Typical Test
Roll Strength + Running [Physical] when sprinting or when difficult running conditions require a test.

Sprinting
Hits increase the distance covered beyond normal running movement for that action.

sneaking.description: Covers moving quietly and remaining unnoticed in different environments.
sneaking.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Sneaking [Physical]. Anyone who might notice you opposes with Intuition + Perception [Mental].

Modifiers
Cover, lighting, noise, movement speed, and environmental conditions may affect either side.

survival.description: Covers finding shelter, food, water, and other necessities while enduring hostile or remote environments.
survival.use:
Typical Test
Roll Willpower + Survival [Mental] against a threshold based on environmental difficulty.

Use For
  • Finding food, water, and shelter.
  • Enduring hostile climates and terrain.
  • Recognising natural hazards and staying alive away from civilisation.

swimming.description: Covers efficient movement, speed, and endurance while swimming.
swimming.use:
Typical Test
Roll Strength + Swimming [Physical] when swimming speed, endurance, rough water, or dangerous conditions matter.

Use For
Surface swimming, staying afloat, and exerting yourself in water.

tracking.description: Covers following people, animals, or vehicles by interpreting tracks and other signs of passage.
tracking.use:
Typical Test
Roll Intuition + Tracking [Mental] against a threshold based on the age and quality of the trail.

Modifiers
Terrain, weather, time, concealment, and the number of subjects may affect the test.

con.description: Covers deception, fast talk, seduction, and convincing others to accept false or misleading claims.
con.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Con [Social] when deceiving someone through lies, fast talk, or manipulation. The target resists with an appropriate social or judgement test.

Use For
  • Fast talk.
  • Bluffing and lying.
  • Distracting or misleading a target.

etiquette.description: Covers understanding and applying the expected behaviour, jargon, and customs of a social group or subculture.
etiquette.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Etiquette [Social] to act appropriately within a social group, make a suitable impression, or avoid a social mistake.

Use For
Knowing expected behaviour, jargon, customs, and protocols for a particular social environment.

impersonation.description: Covers convincingly imitating another person or adopting a false identity through voice, mannerisms, and behaviour.
impersonation.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Impersonation [Social] to convincingly imitate a specific person or type of person. Observers may oppose with Perception, Intuition, or an appropriate social skill.

Note
A physical disguise may require a separate Disguise test.

instruction.description: Covers teaching another character how to perform a skill or understand a subject.
instruction.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Instruction [Social] when teaching another character. Successful instruction can support learning or training when the relevant advancement rules allow it.

Use For
Explaining techniques, coaching practice, and structured training.

intimidation.description: Covers coercing others through threats, fear, interrogation, or displays of force.
intimidation.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Intimidation [Social] against the target's resistance. Circumstances such as obvious threats, superior numbers, weapons, injuries, and social leverage may modify the test.

Use For
Coercing cooperation through fear or threats.

leadership.description: Covers directing, coordinating, inspiring, and rallying other people.
leadership.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Leadership [Social] to direct, inspire, rally, or coordinate others.

Use For
  • Giving clear orders under pressure.
  • Motivating allies.
  • Supporting teamwork when the situation allows.

negotiation.description: Covers bargaining, diplomacy, contract discussion, and reaching favourable agreements.
negotiation.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Negotiation [Social], usually as an Opposed Test against the other negotiator.

Use For
  • Bargaining over payment and prices.
  • Reaching agreements.
  • Resolving disputes through compromise.

performance.description: Covers entertaining or captivating an audience through acting, comedy, dance, music, presentation, or similar performance arts.
performance.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Performance [Social] to entertain or impress an audience. The number of hits determines the quality or impact of the performance.

Use For
Acting, dancing, music, singing, and other live performance.

alchemy.description: Covers creating magical preparations that store spells for later activation.
alchemy.use:
Creating a Preparation
Choose a spell formula and preparation trigger, then roll Magic + Alchemy [Force] as required by the enchanting rules. The resulting preparation stores the spell for later activation.

Drain
The alchemist must resist Drain after creating the preparation.

arcana.description: Covers formal magical theory, including the design and analysis of spells, foci, spirit formulae, and related arcane concepts.
arcana.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Arcana [Mental] for theoretical magical knowledge and tasks that require formal magical understanding.

Use For
  • Designing or analysing magical formulae when the rules allow.
  • Understanding magical theory, traditions, rituals, and phenomena.

artificing.description: Covers creating magical foci and working with the magical structures used to empower them.
artificing.use:
Creating Foci
Use Magic + Artificing with the enchanting rules to create magical foci. The process requires an appropriate formula, telesma, time, and reagents.

Note
Artificing is used for crafting the focus; bonding and using the finished focus follow separate rules.

assensing.description: Covers reading astral auras and signatures to learn information about living beings, magic, and astral phenomena.
assensing.use:
Typical Test
Roll Intuition + Assensing [Astral] while perceiving astrally. More hits reveal increasingly detailed information about an aura, astral form, spirit, spell, or magical object.

Use For
  • Reading emotional and health impressions from auras.
  • Identifying magical signatures and astral qualities.

astral combat.description: Covers fighting while astrally perceiving or projecting, including attacks against spirits, magicians, and astral barriers.
astral combat.use:
Typical Test
Use Logic + Astral Combat [Astral] to attack in astral space. Astral attributes replace physical ones while fully astral; astral damage is based on the attacker's astral Strength equivalent.

Use For
Fighting spirits, projecting magicians, and other astral forms.

banishing.description: Covers disrupting a spirit’s connection to the magician or force that summoned or bound it.
banishing.use:
Typical Test
Roll Magic + Banishing against the spirit's resistance. Net hits remove services owed by the spirit; reducing its remaining services to zero can free it from control.

Drain
The magician resists Drain generated by the banishing attempt.

binding.description: Covers binding a summoned spirit so that it remains available for additional services over a longer period.
binding.use:
Binding a Spirit
After summoning a spirit, perform the binding ritual using Magic + Binding against the spirit's resistance. Binding requires time and reagents.

Result
Net hits determine the additional services the bound spirit owes. The magician must resist Drain from the attempt.

counterspelling.description: Covers protecting targets against hostile spells and disrupting sustained magical effects.
counterspelling.use:
Spell Defence
A magician may use Counterspelling to add protection against hostile spells. Available spell-defence dice are allocated to protected targets and added to applicable defence tests.

Dispelling
Counterspelling is also used when attempting to end an existing sustained spell under the dispelling rules.

disenchanting.description: Covers breaking down or neutralising enchantments such as foci and alchemical preparations.
disenchanting.use:
Typical Use
Use Magic + Disenchanting to disrupt magical preparations, foci, and other enchantments when the enchanting rules permit.

Result
Net hits reduce or break the target enchantment according to its specific rules, and Drain may need to be resisted.

ritual spellcasting.description: Covers performing ritual magic through established ritual procedures and teams.
ritual spellcasting.use:
Performing a Ritual
Use Magic + Ritual Spellcasting [Force] as part of a ritual team. Rituals usually require a leader, participants, a magical lodge, time, and any required link or spotter.

Result
The ritual's final test determines success, and participants may need to resist Drain.

spellcasting.description: Covers casting spells directly through the manipulation of mana.
spellcasting.use:
Casting a Spell
Roll Magic + Spellcasting [Force]. The spell type determines whether the test is opposed, resisted, or made against a threshold.

Drain
After resolving the spell, resist its Drain Value. Drain is Physical when the spell's Force exceeds the caster's Magic rating; otherwise it is Stun.

summoning.description: Covers calling spirits and compelling them to provide services.
summoning.use:
Summoning a Spirit
Choose a spirit type and Force, then roll Magic + Summoning [Force] against the spirit's Force. Net hits become services owed by the spirit.

Drain
Resist Drain equal to twice the spirit's hits, with a minimum Drain Value of 2.

compiling.description: Covers creating sprites from the Resonance for immediate use.
compiling.use:
Compiling a Sprite
Choose a sprite type and Level, then roll Resonance + Compiling [Level] against the sprite's Level. Net hits become tasks owed by the sprite.

Fade
Resist Fade equal to twice the sprite's hits, with a minimum Fade Value of 2.

decompiling.description: Covers weakening and destroying sprites by disrupting their Resonance code.
decompiling.use:
Typical Use
Roll Resonance + Decompiling against a sprite to reduce the tasks it owes and potentially free it from a technomancer's control.

Fade
The technomancer must resist Fade generated by the attempt.

registering.description: Covers registering compiled sprites so they remain available for future services.
registering.use:
Registering a Sprite
After compiling a sprite, spend the required time and roll Resonance + Registering [Level] against the sprite's resistance.

Result
Net hits add tasks and make the sprite registered. The technomancer must resist Fade from the attempt.

aeronautics mechanic.description: Covers diagnosing, maintaining, modifying, and repairing aerospace vehicles and their systems.
aeronautics mechanic.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Aeronautics Mechanic [Mental], often as an Extended Test, to repair, maintain, diagnose, or modify aerospace vehicles and related systems.

Use For
Aircraft, aerospace craft, and associated flight systems where this mechanic skill applies.

animal handling.description: Covers handling, riding, training, and directing animals.
animal handling.use:
Typical Test
Roll Charisma + Animal Handling [Social] to calm, train, command, or work safely with animals.

Modifiers
The animal's training, temperament, injuries, fear, and familiarity with the handler may affect the test.

armorer.description: Covers building, maintaining, modifying, and repairing weapons, armour, and related equipment.
armorer.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Armorer [Mental], often as an Extended Test, to build, repair, maintain, or modify weapons and armour.

Use For
Firearms, melee weapons, armour, ammunition-related work, and similar combat equipment.

artisan.description: Covers creating artistic or crafted works using a chosen discipline such as cooking, drawing, sculpting, fashion, or writing.
artisan.use:
Typical Test
Roll Intuition + Artisan [Mental] to create or evaluate artistic work. Hits determine quality, complexity, or success as appropriate.

Use For
Painting, sculpture, writing, composition, and other creative crafts not covered by Performance.

automotive mechanic.description: Covers diagnosing, maintaining, modifying, and repairing ground vehicles.
automotive mechanic.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Automotive Mechanic [Mental], often as an Extended Test, to diagnose, repair, maintain, or modify ground vehicles and automotive systems.

biotechnology.description: Covers advanced biological technologies such as cloning, gene therapy, bioinformatics, bioware, and vat maintenance.
biotechnology.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Biotechnology [Mental] for technical work involving biological systems, biotech equipment, and laboratory procedures.

Use For
Operating, analysing, repairing, or developing biotechnology when a more specific medical skill is not required.

chemistry.description: Covers analysing and creating chemicals, drugs, toxins, and other chemical compounds.
chemistry.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Chemistry [Mental] to analyse chemical substances or perform laboratory work.

Use For
  • Identifying compounds.
  • Producing or modifying chemicals when proper equipment and materials are available.

computer.description: Covers standard Matrix operations such as searching, editing files, Matrix perception, and legitimate device use.
computer.use:
Matrix Use
Roll Logic + Computer for ordinary Matrix actions such as searching, editing, analysing, and handling data. The Matrix action determines the applicable limit, usually based on a device attribute.

Note
Illegal intrusion actions generally use Hacking or Cybercombat instead.

cybercombat.description: Covers attacking Matrix targets such as devices, personas, IC, and sprites.
cybercombat.use:
Matrix Use
Roll Logic + Cybercombat for aggressive Matrix actions that damage, force access, or attack personas, devices, and icons.

Limit
The specific Matrix action determines the applicable device attribute, commonly Attack.

cybertechnology.description: Covers the technology, installation, maintenance, and repair of cyberware, bioware, and related implants.
cybertechnology.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Cybertechnology [Mental] to install, repair, maintain, or diagnose cyberware and related systems.

Note
Medical implantation procedures may also require appropriate facilities and medical knowledge.

demolitions.description: Covers preparing, placing, identifying, improvising, and defusing explosives.
demolitions.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Demolitions [Mental] to prepare, place, shape, or disarm explosives.

Use For
  • Setting charges for maximum effect.
  • Creating controlled demolitions.
  • Disarming bombs and explosive traps.
Warning
Failure or a glitch can have immediate consequences at the gamemaster's discretion.

electronic warfare.description: Covers communications, encryption, jamming, sensor operations, and related electronic countermeasures.
electronic warfare.use:
Typical Use
Roll Logic + Electronic Warfare for communications interception, jamming, sensor operations, signal analysis, and related electronic-countermeasure tasks.

Limit
The applicable limit depends on the device, sensor, or Matrix action being used.

first aid.description: Covers immediate emergency treatment of injuries and stabilisation of wounded characters.
first aid.use:
Emergency Treatment
Roll Logic + First Aid [Mental] (2) soon after a character is injured. Hits beyond the threshold heal boxes of damage, subject to the normal First Aid limits and modifiers.

Modifiers
Wounds, poor conditions, inadequate supplies, implants, and other circumstances can change the dice pool.

Note
First Aid is immediate treatment; long-term care uses Medicine.

forgery.description: Covers creating convincing counterfeit documents, credentials, images, and similar false records.
forgery.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Forgery [Mental] to create or alter false documents, credentials, signatures, or similar physical records.

Opposition
A forged item may later be checked with Perception, technical analysis, or another suitable test.

hacking.description: Covers bypassing Matrix security and illegally manipulating devices, files, hosts, and personas.
hacking.use:
Matrix Use
Roll Logic + Hacking for illegal Matrix intrusion that relies on stealth, deception, or exploiting security.

Common Use
Actions such as Hack on the Fly use Hacking and are limited by the relevant Matrix attribute, commonly Sleaze.

hardware.description: Covers diagnosing, modifying, and repairing electronic devices and computer hardware.
hardware.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Hardware [Mental], often as an Extended Test, to build, repair, modify, or physically bypass electronic devices and Matrix hardware.

Use For
Commlinks, cyberdecks, electronics, circuitry, and physical access to device components.

industrial mechanic.description: Covers diagnosing, maintaining, modifying, and repairing industrial machinery and large mechanical systems.
industrial mechanic.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Industrial Mechanic [Mental], often as an Extended Test, to diagnose, repair, maintain, or modify industrial machinery and heavy mechanical systems.

locksmith.description: Covers defeating, servicing, and manipulating mechanical and electronic locks and access-control hardware.
locksmith.use:
Typical Test
Roll Agility + Locksmith [Physical] to manipulate mechanical locks and security hardware by hand. Electronic locks may also require technical or Matrix skills depending on the method used.

Use For
Picking locks, bypassing mechanical security, and working with lock mechanisms.

medicine.description: Covers advanced medical diagnosis, surgery, long-term care, and professional treatment.
medicine.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Medicine [Mental] for diagnosis, treatment, surgery, and long-term medical care.

Use For
  • Supporting natural recovery.
  • Treating conditions beyond immediate First Aid.
  • Performing medical procedures with appropriate equipment and facilities.

nautical mechanic.description: Covers diagnosing, maintaining, modifying, and repairing boats, ships, submarines, and other watercraft.
nautical mechanic.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Nautical Mechanic [Mental], often as an Extended Test, to diagnose, repair, maintain, or modify boats, ships, submarines, and related marine systems.

navigation.description: Covers route planning and finding direction using maps, landmarks, compasses, GPS, and other navigation aids.
navigation.use:
Typical Test
Roll Intuition + Navigation [Mental] to determine position, choose a route, avoid becoming lost, or navigate difficult terrain.

Modifiers
Maps, GPS, visibility, terrain, weather, and familiarity with the area may affect the test.

software.description: Covers creating, analysing, and manipulating programs, code, data bombs, and complex software systems.
software.use:
Typical Test
Roll Logic + Software [Mental] to write, analyse, alter, or repair software when the task is not resolved by a normal Matrix action.

Use For
Programming, code analysis, software development, and specialised technical software tasks.

gunnery.description: Covers firing vehicle-mounted, drone-mounted, remote-operated, and turreted weapons.
gunnery.use:
Vehicle Weapon Attacks
Use Gunnery to fire mounted weapons on vehicles and drones. The linked attribute depends on how the weapon is controlled: direct manual operation normally uses Agility, while remote operation may use Logic.

Limit
Use the weapon's Accuracy or the relevant vehicle/sensor limit as required by the control method.

pilot aerospace.description: Covers piloting spacecraft, launch craft, suborbital vehicles, and other aerospace vehicles.
pilot aerospace.use:
Typical Test
Roll Reaction + Pilot Aerospace [Handling] when piloting aerospace vehicles under conditions that require a test.

Use For
Routine control, difficult manoeuvres, chases, and Crash Tests involving aerospace craft.

pilot aircraft.description: Covers piloting fixed-wing, rotary-wing, lighter-than-air, tilt-wing, and vectored-thrust aircraft.
pilot aircraft.use:
Typical Test
Roll Reaction + Pilot Aircraft [Handling] when flying aircraft under conditions that require a test.

Use For
Take-off, landing, difficult manoeuvres, chases, and Crash Tests.

pilot walker.description: Covers piloting bipedal, quadrupedal, multipedal, and other walker vehicles.
pilot walker.use:
Typical Test
Roll Reaction + Pilot Walker [Handling] to control walker vehicles and similar legged machines.

Use For
Difficult manoeuvres, chases, hazardous terrain, and Crash Tests.

pilot exotic vehicle.description: Covers piloting one specific unusual vehicle type that is not governed by another standard Pilot skill.
pilot exotic vehicle.use:
Specialisation Required
This skill applies to one specific exotic vehicle type. Each unrelated vehicle type requires its own Pilot Exotic Vehicle skill.

Typical Test
Roll Reaction + Pilot Exotic Vehicle [Handling].

Defaulting
This skill cannot be defaulted.

pilot ground craft.description: Covers piloting bikes, cars, trucks, hovercraft, tracked vehicles, and other ground vehicles.
pilot ground craft.use:
Typical Test
Roll Reaction + Pilot Ground Craft [Handling] when driving ground vehicles under conditions that require a test.

Use For
Difficult manoeuvres, chases, hazardous terrain, and Crash Tests involving cars, bikes, trucks, and similar vehicles.

pilot watercraft.description: Covers piloting motorboats, sailboats, ships, submarines, hydrofoils, and other watercraft.
pilot watercraft.use:
Typical Test
Roll Reaction + Pilot Watercraft [Handling] when controlling boats, ships, and other watercraft under conditions that require a test.

Use For
Difficult manoeuvres, chases, rough conditions, and Crash Tests.
